zh.lifeSummary: 當John Montgomery of Eaglesham and Eastwood 7th Lord of Eaglesham於1250年出生在蘇格蘭雷福福爾易阿格萊善姆時，他的父親John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham26歲，母親Margaret Moray of Bothwell30歲。他於大約1300年與Janet Erskine在蘇格蘭雷福福爾易阿格萊善姆結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1328年在他的家鄉享年78歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭雷福福爾易阿格萊善姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Montgomery of Eaglesham and Eastwood 7th Lord of Eaglesham于1250年出生在苏格兰雷福福爾易阿格萊善姆时，他的父亲John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham26岁，母亲Margaret Moray of Bothwell30岁。他于大约1300年与Janet Erskine在苏格兰雷福福爾易阿格萊善姆结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1328年在他的家乡享年78岁，埋葬在苏格兰雷福福爾易阿格萊善姆。

nameMeanings.name: Montgomery
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and northern Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Sainte-Foy-de-Montgomery and Saint-Germain-de-Montgomery (Calvados). In Ireland this surname was present in the medieval period, died out, and was then reintroduced from Scotland in the 17th century. It has been Gaelicized in Ireland as Mac Iomaire and in Scotland as Mac Gumaraid.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Eaglesham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Scottish: locative name from Eaglesham (Renfrews), a parish and former barony.

variants.name: Eagleson
meanings: Scottish: altered form of Eaglesham, a habitational name from a parish and former barony so named in Renfrewshire.

variants.name: Eaglesome
meanings: Scottish:  see  Eaglesham  .

nameMeanings.name: and
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Ali, Hossein, Abbas, Behnaz, Gitti, Hamid, Jafar, Mansour, Masoud, Mehdi, Pari, Rana.  Jewish Jechiel, Bracha, Herschel, Morry.
Iranian: from Zand, the name of a dinasty which ruled Persia 1751–94. It is based on Middle Persian zand ‘great, powerful’ and was originally the name of a former Iranian tribe.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Sand  .

variants.name: Sand
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Bertel, Ejnar, Lars, Levinus, Nels, Niels, Thor.

English, Danish, Norwegian, Swedish, German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): topographic name for someone who lived on or near a patch of sandy soil, from the vocabulary word sand (German Sand). As a Swedish name it was often purely ornamental, and as a Jewish artificial. It is also found in France (see 3 below).
English: habitational name from a place such as Send (Surrey) or Sound (Cheshire), named with Middle English sand, sond ‘sand’.

variants.name: Zant
meanings: German: variant of   Zand  .
Dutch: variant of   Van Zandt  .

variants.name: Zandi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Mahmud, Taher, Ahmad, Azi, Bahram, Behzad, Farman, Firooz, Hamid, Hossein, Jaleh, Masood.
Iranian: derivative of   Zand  , denoting descent from or association with a Persian dinasty of that name.
Italian (Emilia): perhaps a nickname from the dialect word gianda ‘acorn’.

nameMeanings.name: Eastwood
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (mainly Yorkshire and Lancashire): habitational name from any of various places called Eastwood, such as in Keighley, Rotherham, or Todmorden (all Yorkshire), or Eastwood in Nottinghamshire. Most of these placenames come from Old English ēast ‘east’ + wudu ‘wood’, but Eastwood in Nottinghamshire originally had as its final element Old Norse thveit ‘clearing’ (see Thwaites ). Compare Astwood .
English (Devon): from Eastwood in Peters Marland or Inwardleigh (both Devon), named with Middle English bi este wode ‘(place) to the east of the wood’.
Probably also an Americanized form (translation into English) of Dutch Oostwoud: habitational name from the village Oostwoud in the province of North Holland.

variants.name: Astwood
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the places called Astwood in Buckinghamshire and Worcestershire, named with Old English ēast ‘east’ + wudu ‘wood’, or from Astwith (Derbyshire), named with Old English ēast ‘east’ + Old Norse thveit ‘clearing’. Compare Eastwood , of which this name may also be a variant.

variants.name: Thwaites
meanings: English: from the northern English placename Thwaite (Old Norse thveit ‘meadow, paddock’) + plural or post-medieval excrescent -s. The plural form is found in several placenames, including Thwaites in Keighley (Yorkshire), which is a principal source of the surname.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

en.lifeSummary: When John Montgomery of Eaglesham and Eastwood 7th Lord of Eaglesham was born in 1250, in Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Scotland, his father, John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham, was 26 and his mother, Margaret Moray of Bothwell, was 30. He married Janet Erskine about 1300, in Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1328, in his hometown, at the age of 78, and was buried in Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Montgomery of Eaglesham and Eastwood 7th Lord of Eaglesham est né en 1250, à Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Écosse, son père, John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Margaret Moray of Bothwell, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Janet Erskine environ 1300, à Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1328, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 78 ans, et a été enterré à Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als John Montgomery of Eaglesham and Eastwood 7th Lord of Eaglesham 1250 in Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham 26 und seine Mutter Margaret Moray of Bothwell 30. Er hat um 1300 in Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Scotland Janet Erskine geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1328 im Alter von 78 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John Montgomery of Eaglesham and Eastwood 7th Lord of Eaglesham è nato il nel 1250 nel Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Scotland, il suo padre, John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Moray of Bothwell, aveva 30 anni. Lui sposato Janet Erskine circa 1300, nel Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1328, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 78 anni, e fu sepolto a Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: John Montgomery of Eaglesham and Eastwood 7th Lord of Eagleshamが1250年にスコットランドレンフリューシュアイアエアグレシャムで生まれた時，父John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eagleshamは26歳，母Margaret Moray of Bothwellは30歳であった。約1300年にスコットランドレンフリューシュアイアエアグレシャムでJanet Erskineと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1328年に故郷で78歳で死去し，スコットランドレンフリューシュアイアエアグレシャムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John Montgomery of Eaglesham and Eastwood 7th Lord of Eaglesham(이)가 1250년 스컷랜드 렌프레w실르 이글레샤에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Moray of Bothwell(은)는 30세였다. 그는 대략 1300년 스컷랜드 렌프레w실르 이글레샤에서 Janet Erskine(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1328년 그의 고향에서 78세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 렌프레w실르 이글레샤에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Montgomery of Eaglesham and Eastwood 7th Lord of Eaglesham nasceu em 1250, em Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Scotland, seu pai, John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Moray of Bothwell, tinha 30 anos. Ele casou-se com Janet Erskine aproximadamente 1300, em Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1328, em sua cidade natal, com 78 anos, e foi sepultado em Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Montgomery of Eaglesham and Eastwood 7th Lord of Eaglesham родился в 1250 г. в местности эаглешам, ренфрюшир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Moray of Bothwell, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Janet Erskine около 1300 г. в местности эаглешам, ренфрюшир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1328 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 78 лет и был похоронен в местности эаглешам, ренфрюшир, Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Montgomery of Eaglesham and Eastwood 7th Lord of Eaglesham nació en 1250, en Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Scotland, su papá, John Montgomery "Miles," of Eaglesham 3rd Laird of Eaglesham, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Margaret Moray of Bothwell, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Janet Erskine aproximadamente 1300, en Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1328, en su pueblo, a la edad de 78 años, y fue sepultado en Eaglesham, Renfrewshire, Scotland.
